food_subgroup: Processed meat products — chicken frankfurter / hot dog in bun with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002516 covers hot dog food products generically; no specific entry exists for chicken hot dog meal sets from Jollibee. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hot dog/frankfurter sandwiches generically. No specific code exists for a chicken-based hot dog meal set. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for a chicken hot dog fast-food meal set product.

serving.description: 1 meal set (hot dog in bun + side + drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Jollibee Jolly Chicken Hot Dog Meal (D) set comprising a chicken frankfurter in a steamed bun with condiments, a side of french fries or rice, and a soft drink, estimated at approximately 450 g total edible weight.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Hot dog, chicken, plain on bun; supplemented with USDA SR Legacy frankfurter and bun component data for meal set estimation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for the Jollibee Jolly Chicken Hot Dog Meal (D) set as sold in Singapore. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by component-weighted averaging: chicken frankfurter (USDA FDC #172949, chicken frankfurter), enriched hot dog bun (USDA FDC #325871), standard condiments (ketchup, mustard, mayonnaise), and a representative side of french fries (USDA FDC #170720). The meal set is estimated at ~450 g total. Per-100 g values are derived from this composite. Values carry medium confidence due to estimation from components rather than direct laboratory analysis of the Jollibee product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — hot dog / frankfurter in white bread bun analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Jolly Chicken Hot Dog Meal (D). GI of 60 is estimated based on the analogous food 'hot dog in white bread bun' from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. The white enriched bun is the dominant glycemic driver (GI ~70–75 for white bread); the chicken frankfurter and condiments lower the composite GI. A blended GI of 60 (medium) is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (60 × 127.8 g carbohydrate in 450 g serving) / 100 ≈ 76.7; however, the side component (fries or rice) contributes substantially. For the hot dog component alone (~150 g, ~42.6 g carbohydrate): GL = (60 × 42.6) / 100 = 25.6. Full meal set GL reported as 38.5 using weighted composite carbohydrate of 64.2 g available carbohydrate across the full 450 g set at GI 60: (60 × 64.2) / 100 = 38.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2340 mg per full meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set due to its very high sodium content, which substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg sodium per meal threshold; if consumed, pair with a low-sodium remainder of the day and avoid adding condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~38.5 per meal set) driven by refined white bun, fries or rice side, and soft drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this meal set due to its high glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content; if consumed, substitute the soft drink with water, choose a rice side over fries, and monitor postprandial blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and ultra-processed chicken frankfurter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set due to its high sodium (~2340 mg per set), saturated fat (~12.6 g per set), and the presence of trans fat and ultra-processed meat, all of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jolly Chicken Hot Dog Meal (D) is a fast-food meal set sold by Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Jollibee Bugis Village. The meal consists of a steamed or soft enriched white bread bun encasing a grilled or steamed chicken frankfurter (a processed chicken sausage), dressed with condiments such as ketchup, mustard, and mayonnaise, served alongside a side dish (typically french fries or steamed rice) and a soft drink. The chicken frankfurter is made from mechanically separated or minced chicken meat combined with starch, salt, spices, and preservatives, making it an ultra-processed meat product. The bun is soft and slightly sweet in texture, characteristic of Filipino-style hot dog buns. The overall flavour profile is savoury and mildly sweet, with a soft, yielding texture from the bun and a juicy, slightly smoky sausage. Nutritionally, the meal set is energy-dense (~238 kcal per 100 g; ~1071 kcal per 450 g set), high in refined carbohydrates, sodium (~520 mg per 100 g), and saturated fat. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), but the glycemic load per full meal set is high (~38.5) due to the large carbohydrate portion from the bun, fries or rice, and soft drink. The meal is certified Halal and is free from dairy. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, high glycemic load, saturated fat, and ultra-processed meat content. It is best consumed occasionally and in moderation within a balanced dietary pattern.

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory analysis or official nutritional disclosure from Jollibee Singapore for this specific meal set was available from approved sources. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA FoodData Central data for chicken frankfurter, enriched hot dog bun, condiments, and french fries. GI is estimated from the analogous 'hot dog in white bread bun' entry in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct product-specific approved-source entry.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries (chicken frankfurter FDC #172949, enriched hot dog bun FDC #325871, french fries FDC #170720, standard condiments) and represent a weighted per-100 g composite for the full meal set (~450 g). Direct Jollibee Singapore nutritional disclosure was not available from any approved source at time of processing. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this estimation. 3. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on the hot dog in white bread bun reference from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this Jollibee product exists in approved sources. 4. The meal set composition (hot dog + side + drink) may vary by outlet or promotional period; the (D) suffix likely denotes a specific combo variant. 5. Trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g based on USDA data for similar processed chicken frankfurter products; this is a trace level. 6. The food is tagged Halal per Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification as listed on field survey data cuisine tags; this is consistent with Jollibee Singapore's known Halal status. 7. Ontology codes use closest_parent match types as no exact codes exist for this specific branded meal set product.
